user_correction:
Remaining improvement areas 
Transcript directory resolution is fragile — Both run_evolution.py and evolution_maintenance_hook.py use the same pattern: iterate ~/.claude/projects/ subdirs, pick the first one with .jsonl files. If the user has multiple Claude projects, only the first alphabetically gets mined. The others are silently ignored. Consider mining all subdirs, or letting the user configure which project to mine via config.json.

deploy_optimization uses str.replace() for applying changes — change.original is matched via simple string replacement. If the same phrase appears twice in the skill body, only the first occurrence is replaced (replace(..., 1)). This is correct for the "remove" case but could be surprising for "replace" — the user correction might apply to the second occurrence. Low risk since skill files are typically short.

_log_to_evolution searches two hardcoded candidate paths for EVOLUTION.md — skills_dir.parent.parent / ".context" and skills_dir.parent / ".context". This works for the current directory layout but is brittle if the skills directory is ever relocated. Could use the same config-based workspace path resolution as other modules.

recall_about relevance ranking sorts by (topic_lower in content, len(content)) — The boolean topic_lower in content is a substring match, not a word boundary match. A message containing "kubernetes" will rank the same as one containing "mykubernetescluster". Minor, but a \b word boundary check would be more precise.

No rollback mechanism for deploy_optimization — If a deployed skill change causes problems, there's no automated way to revert. The EVOLUTION.md log records what changed, but the original SKILL.md content isn't preserved. A simple backup (e.g., SKILL.md.bak or git commit before deploy) would make this safer.
